description_long: This object is a classic A-frame traffic barricade, commonly used for roadwork and pedestrian control in urban environments. It features two rectangular wooden panels, one above the other, supported by a pair of angled legs on each side, forming a stable freestanding structure. The upper panel is marked with bold diagonal orange and white reflective stripes, serving as a high-visibility warning to passersby and vehicles. The wood appears weathered and slightly worn, indicating outdoor use and exposure to the elements. The structure is held together by visible bolts or screws, and the legs are splayed to provide stability. There are no visible lights or additional signage, and the object is designed to be easily movable by a single person.
description: A weathered wooden A-frame traffic barricade with orange and white diagonal stripes and two support panels, designed for high visibility and stability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the orange and white striped upper panel and the plain lower panel, both supported by angled legs.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the barricade and the A-frame structure formed by the angled legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the unpainted back of the panels and the same structural layout as the front, but without the reflective stripes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the A-frame construction.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal orange and white stripes, wide, repeating across the upper panel.
